notes:
San Carlos has no existing page/baseline on this site yet, so every venue below is matchStatus 'new'. Pickleheads' own city-level stats say '3 pickleball locations with 11 courts' in San Carlos, broken down as '1 indoor location with 3 indoor courts' + '2 outdoor locations with 8 outdoor courts'. Two of the three are directly named and linked in search results (Highlands Park, San Carlos Youth Center); the third is never directly linked in any search result obtained (pickleheads.com returns HTTP 403 on direct WebFetch, same block prior research sessions in this repo already documented, so only WebSearch-indexed/cached content was available) but is confirmed by process of elimination to be Crestview Park — the only other entry on the city's own official 'PICKLEBALL COURT LOCATIONS' list, and the only remaining candidate after ruling out every other 'San Carlos'-named result (see cross-contamination warning below). A fourth real, currently-operating public venue — Burton Park — turned up repeatedly across five independent pickleball-court aggregators and the city's own (separate) Tennis Courts page, but is NOT on Pickleheads and NOT on the city's dedicated Pickleball program page; it's included here per this task's broader 'every real public venue' scope, with confirmed sourcing noted per-venue. Total: 4 real venues found, 0 excluded-but-real.

CROSS-CONTAMINATION WARNING — 'San Carlos' collides hard with unrelated places across nearly every source used, exactly the kind of trap flagged in this repo's San Mateo research file (Westborough Park incident). Explicitly ruled OUT of this file: (1) 'San Carlos Pickleball Club' / sancarlospickleball.com — this is a real, well-documented, 14-court club, but it is in San Carlos, SONORA, MEXICO (a Gulf-of-California resort town near Guaymas), not San Mateo County. Giveaways once you look closely: peso-denominated fees ('Tactica Tuesday: 100 pesos'), Spanish event names, and a second source giving its address as 'Ranchitos San Carlos, Sonora, 85506, Mexico'. It dominates search results for 'San Carlos Pickleball Club' and would be an easy false positive. (2) 'Athletic Club San Carlos' — a Pickleheads listing, but geocoded by Pickleheads itself to Menlo Park, CA (3 Callie Ln) in one search pass and to San Carlos, Sonora, Mexico in another; neither is San Carlos, CA, so excluded either way. (3) 'San Carlos Pickleball Association' — a Pickleheads listing located in Nuevo, CA (Riverside County, Southern California), unrelated to Bay Area San Carlos; a same-named community Facebook group may well be a real local group for the Bay Area city, but it's a group, not a venue, so it wouldn't need its own record regardless. (4) 'San Carlo Park' (Irvine, CA — different spelling/city) and 'Carl Larsen Playground' (San Francisco) — unrelated, surfaced only on generic keyword overlap. No dedicated Bay-Area San Carlos membership pickleball club was found distinct from the 4 public venues below.

Also note: 'North Crestview Park' is a separate, still-undeveloped 4.3-acre passive/nature park parcel (walking paths, birdwatching, Bay views) whose master-plan concept was only approved by City Council in 2025; the city's own parks index explicitly describes it as a passive park, not a sports-court venue. It is NOT the same place as the existing, developed 'Crestview Park' at 1000 Crestview Drive covered below — don't conflate the two in any future pass. Separately, San Carlos's 'Highlands Park' (2600 Melendy Drive) is unrelated to the 'Highlands Recreation District' (highlandsrec.ca.gov), a different special district serving the unincorporated San Mateo Highlands/North Fair Oaks area.

Time-sensitive as of this research date: the city states San Carlos Youth Center's indoor pickleball is 'currently paused for summer camps' and resumes in September — see that venue's notes.

venues.name: Highlands Park
venues.surface: Outdoor hard court, permanent pickleball lines, portable nets
venues.hours: Pickleball has priority on Courts 1 & 2 daily 9am-12pm and Mon-Fri 5pm-10pm; first-come-first-served at all other times; closed the first Thursday of each month for maintenance
venues.weather: Mild, sunny mid-Peninsula climate — warmer and less foggy than SF/coastal areas; dry summers, occasional morning marine layer near the Bay flats
venues.notes: The city's own Pickleball page (primary source) gives the address and a detailed priority-hours schedule verbatim but does not itself state a court count; Pickleheads, Places2Play, and TennisPickleballCalifornia all independently agree on 4 outdoor courts, so that number is used here — treated as confirmed on that basis. The city's text specifies pickleball 'only takes priority on Courts 1 & 2' during the posted windows, with 'all other times not listed... on first come first serve'; one aggregator separately describes the site as having up to 5 tennis courts. The city doesn't spell out exactly how 'Courts 1 & 2' maps onto the 4 pickleball-striped courts, so treat that as a scheduling nuance (2 of the courts get guaranteed pickleball priority in the listed windows) rather than a literal 4-vs-2 conflict — all 4 are pickleball-usable, just not all with scheduled priority. Not to be confused with the separate 'Highlands Recreation District' (highlandsrec.ca.gov), an unrelated special district serving the unincorporated San Mateo Highlands/North Fair Oaks area — see top-level notes.

venues.name: Crestview Park
venues.surface: Not specified (city's general parks page describes the park generically as having 'sports courts' among a grassy field, picnic areas, and a modern playground; no source states surface material)
venues.weather: Mild, sunny mid-Peninsula climate — warmer and less foggy than SF/coastal areas; dry summers, occasional morning marine layer near the Bay flats
venues.notes: Listed by the city's own Parks & Recreation Pickleball page as one of exactly 3 official pickleball court locations. Quoting the raw fetched page in full for this entry: 'Crestview Park / 1000 Crestview Drive / San Carlos, CA 94070' — nothing else (no hours, no fee, no court count). The city's general Parks & Facilities page separately calls it a 7-acre park with 'sports courts' (type/count unspecified) plus a grassy field, picnic areas, and a modern playground. No aggregator checked (Pickleheads, Places2Play, Pickleballify, GlobalPickleball.network, Bounce, TennisPickleballCalifornia) publishes a dedicated per-venue page or a direct court-count statement for this specific park. The courts figure used here (4) is NOT a directly stated fact for this venue — it's arithmetic: Pickleheads' city-level stats state '2 outdoor locations with 8 outdoor courts' in San Carlos, and Pickleheads directly states Highlands Park (the other outdoor location) has 4 courts, implying Crestview accounts for the remaining 4 by subtraction. Flagging this explicitly rather than presenting it as sourced fact — confirmed:false and a human should verify directly. Separately, a WebSearch synthesis pass at one point asserted Crestview has courts '#1-2' with specific Wed/Fri tennis-instruction hours; checked against the raw fetched city page text and found NO such statement for Crestview — this looks like the search AI conflating the 'Courts 1 & 2' language from the Highlands Park section of the same source page. Do not carry that claim forward. Do not confuse this park with 'North Crestview Park,' a separate, still-undeveloped 4.3-acre passive/nature parcel elsewhere in the city — see top-level notes. Coordinates come from a single non-pickleball-specific geocoder (mypacer.com), not cross-confirmed by a second source, hence approxLocation:true. No numeric Google Maps cid found.

venues.name: San Carlos Youth Center
venues.surface: Indoor hard and wood court (gymnasium), permanent lines, portable nets
venues.price: $5 drop-in fee, paid at the Youth Center
venues.hours: Tuesdays and Fridays 9am-12pm — but currently paused for summer camps as of this research date (July 2026); city states indoor pickleball resumes in September
venues.weather: Mild, sunny mid-Peninsula climate — warmer and less foggy than SF/coastal areas; dry summers, occasional morning marine layer near the Bay flats
venues.notes: City's own Pickleball page (primary source), quoted verbatim: 'San Carlos Youth Center (Indoor) — currently paused for summer camps. Indoor pickleball will resume in September. 1001 Chestnut Street, San Carlos, CA 94070. Open Tuesdays and Fridays from 9 a.m. - 12 p.m. $5 drop in fee that can be paid at the Youth Center.' As of this research date, indoor pickleball here is on a summer hiatus per the city itself — confirm resumption before planning a visit. Court count: Pickleheads and Places2Play both independently state 3 indoor hard/wood courts; Bounce states 2 — an unresolved conflict, so confirmed:false. (Bounce's own San Carlos listing separately, and wrongly, folded in a Sonora-Mexico venue as if it were local, which weakens confidence in its count here, but the conflict is still recorded rather than silently overridden.) Minor secondary hours conflict, not the primary field value used: Places2Play states 'Tuesdays 10:30 AM-12:30 PM' with no Friday session, disagreeing with the city's own Tuesdays-and-Fridays-9-to-noon schedule; the city's text is treated as authoritative for the hours field per this task's sourcing priority. The Youth Center building is reported by other sources to sit inside Burton Park's grounds, consistent with its geocoded coordinates landing almost on top of Burton Park's (see that venue's notes on the shared block). Lights not marked true despite being an indoor venue, since no source explicitly lists it as a stated amenity (only 'restrooms' was). Coordinates from a single address-lookup source, not cross-confirmed by a second independent geocoder, hence approxLocation:true. Only a Google Maps place_id (not a numeric cid) was found for this venue, so per this task's rules googleMapsUrl is left null rather than using a worse-format link.

venues.name: Burton Park
venues.surface: Outdoor concrete, temporary (non-permanent) pickleball lines painted over shared tennis courts, bring-your-own net
venues.netType: Bring-your-own portable net on temporary lines
venues.hours: Not posted specifically for pickleball (no scheduled priority-hours program); general park hours reported elsewhere as approximately 7am-9pm daily; city's Tennis Courts page separately states court lights are available until 10pm (button-activated, on for 1 hour per press)
venues.weather: Mild, sunny mid-Peninsula climate — warmer and less foggy than SF/coastal areas; dry summers, occasional morning marine layer near the Bay flats
venues.notes: Not listed on the city's dedicated Pickleball program page (which names only Highlands Park, Crestview Park, and the Youth Center) — instead documented on the city's separate Tennis Courts page ('Burton Park and Highlands Tennis Courts Rules'), which confirms real, numbered, lit courts exist here (quoted verbatim: 'The button to turn on the lights at Burton Courts is located in the fence on Court #2') without itself stating a pickleball-specific count. Five independent pickleball-specific aggregators (Pickleballify, Places2Play, GlobalPickleball.network, Bounce, TennisPickleballCalifornia) all independently agree: 3 outdoor concrete courts, temporary (not permanent) pickleball lines, bring-your-own-net, free, no reservation — treated as confirmed on that basis despite being outside the city's formal pickleball program (no scheduled priority hours the way Highlands has; this is shared, first-come tennis-court space the pickleball community has adopted informally, per Places2Play noting 'courts are not specifically designated, informal setup'). Address conflict, NOT treated as disqualifying (same pattern seen in this repo's existing San Mateo research for large multi-frontage parks, e.g. Beresford Park): pickleball-specific sources consistently cite 1017 Cedar St, while Yelp, Waze, and GlobalPickleball.network instead give 900 Chestnut St for the same park. Most likely explanation: this 10-acre park's block spans both Cedar St and Chestnut St — the Youth Center's own address (1001 Chestnut St, see that venue's entry) is reported to sit inside this same park, and its geocoded coordinates land almost on top of Burton Park's. 1017 Cedar St is used here since every pickleball-specific source cites it. googleMapsUrl's cid (6664665096482746472) and lat/lon come from a page that explicitly labels itself 'Google Maps Link' for this venue and are corroborated closely (within ~5 meters) by GlobalPickleball.network's independently listed coordinates, hence approxLocation:false.
